title: French MPs pass law banning social media for under-15s
subhead: Lawmakers voted through a new law that introduces a broad ban on social media access for children under 15, without specifying a list of targeted platforms. The law also bans mobile phones in high schools.

'Stopping AI because it carries risks? That would be like banning cars because they cause accidents'
subhead: The challenge for humans is not choosing between accelerating or slowing down artificial intelligence, but creating the conditions for public trust, argue entrepreneur Eric Hazan and economist Olivier Sibony for Le Monde.

title: Sleeping Beauty syndrome: 'For a week, I slept 15 to 20 hours out of every 24'
subhead: This extremely rare condition, also known as Kleine-Levin syndrome (KLS), mainly affects boys. It is characterized by episodes of massive hypersomnia lasting one or more weeks, often accompanied by cognitive and sometimes behavioral disturbances.

title: 'The country is underrated': Tourists flock to Ethiopia despite ongoing security concerns
subhead: In just nine months, the number of foreign visitors to Ethiopia increased by 15% – the fastest growth rate on the African continent. The government, which has made tourism a key pillar of its economy despite insecurity in some regions, has highlighted these figures.

title: Copenhagen emerges as new pop laboratory
subhead: A new Danish scene, made up mostly of women, has been offering an alternative, minimalist and sensual pop for several years. Many of the musicians, who often trained at the city's conservatory, have taken their music beyond the borders of Scandinavia.

Bernard Arnault, a 'free-marketeer' who whispers in the ear of every president
subhead: 'Bernard Arnault's Empire' (2/6). LVMH's boss has never hidden his allegiance to France's pro-business, establishment right. He's close to fellow billionaire Vincent Bolloré and, above all, pragmatic. Now he's preparing for the far right's rise to power.

title: In Paris café, terror attack survivors find community: 'People want to tell each other: I am alive'
subhead: After long days attending the 2015 Paris attacks trial, survivors and relatives of victims met in a bar to comfort one another. They have continued to gather every month, joined by victims of other attacks, to share experiences, advice and, above all, laughter and good company.

title: 20 men, a 'sperm party,' and two days of sexual torment: The brutality of the 'libertine' rapes case
subhead: Over the course of a long weekend, one of Christophe B.'s former partners was forced to endure sexual assaults by numerous men at their home. Some of these assaults were broadcast live on the swinging website Wyylde.
